title: Homemade Bread
ingredients: 1/4 cup honey or sugar ((85g honey, 50g sugar))
steps: Prepare the dough: In a large bowl or stand mixer add the yeast, water and a pinch of the sugar or honey.
Allow to rest for 5-10 minutes until foaming and bubbly. (This is called “proofing” the yeast, to make sure it is active.
Add remaining sugar or honey, salt, oil, and 3 cups of flour.
Mix to combine, Add remaining sugar or honey, salt, oil, and 3 cups of flour.
Mix to combine, Add another cup of flour and mix to combine.
With the mixer running add more flour, ½ cup at a time, until the dough begins to pull away from the sides of the bowl.
Add a little more flour, if needed.
Add another cup of flour and mix to combine.
Knead the dough: Mix the dough for 4-5 minutes on medium speed (or knead with your hands on a lightly floured surface, for 5-8 minutes).
First Rise: Grease a large bowl with oil or cooking spray and place the dough inside, turning to coat.
Cover with a dish towel or plastic wrap and allow to rise in a warm place* until doubled in size, about 1 1/2 hours.
Punch the dough down well to remove air bubbles.
Second rise, Cover pans with a lightweight, Dry dish towel (or spray two pieces of plastic wrap generously with cooking spray
Lay them gently over the pans), Allow dough to rise again for about 45 minutes to one hour, or until risen about 1 inch above the loaf pans.
Bake: Preheat oven to 350 F, Bake bread for about 30-33 minutes, or until golden brown on top.
Brush the tops with butter and allow to cool for at least 10 minutes before slicing.

rawInstructions: Prepare the dough: In a large bowl or stand mixer add the yeast, water and a pinch of the sugar or honey.
Allow to rest for 5-10 minutes until foaming and bubbly. (This is called “proofing” the yeast, to make sure it is active.
If it doesn’t foam, the yeast is no good, and you need to start over with fresh yeast).
Prepare the dough: In a large bowl or stand mixer add the yeast, water and a pinch of the sugar or honey.
Allow to rest for 5-10 minutes until foaming and bubbly. (This is called “proofing” the yeast, to make sure it is active.
If it doesn’t foam, the yeast is no good, and you need to start over with fresh yeast).
Add remaining sugar or honey, salt, oil, and 3 cups of flour.
Mix to combine.
Add remaining sugar or honey, salt, oil, and 3 cups of flour.
Mix to combine.
Add another cup of flour and mix to combine.
With the mixer running add more flour, ½ cup at a time, until the dough begins to pull away from the sides of the bowl.
The dough should be smooth and elastic, and slightly stick to a clean finger, but not be overly sticky.
Add a little more flour, if needed.
Add another cup of flour and mix to combine.
With the mixer running add more flour, ½ cup at a time, until the dough begins to pull away from the sides of the bowl.
The dough should be smooth and elastic, and slightly stick to a clean finger, but not be overly sticky.
Add a little more flour, if needed.
Knead the dough: Mix the dough for 4-5 minutes on medium speed (or knead with your hands on a lightly floured surface, for 5-8 minutes).

First Rise: Grease a large bowl with oil or cooking spray and place the dough inside, turning to coat.
Cover with a dish towel or plastic wrap and allow to rise in a warm place* until doubled in size, about 1 1/2 hours.
First Rise: Grease a large bowl with oil or cooking spray and place the dough inside, turning to coat.
Cover with a dish towel or plastic wrap and allow to rise in a warm place* until doubled in size, about 1 1/2 hours.
Spray two 9x5'' bread pans generously with cooking spray on all sides. (I also like to line the bottom of the pans with a small piece of parchment paper, but this is optional.)
Spray two 9x5'' bread pans generously with cooking spray on all sides. (I also like to line the bottom of the pans with a small piece of parchment paper, but this is optional.)
Punch the dough down well to remove air bubbles.
Divide into two equal portions.
Shape each ball into long logs and place into greased loaf pans.
Punch the dough down well to remove air bubbles.
Divide into two equal portions.
Shape each ball into long logs and place into greased loaf pans.
Second rise: Cover pans with a lightweight, dry dish towel (or spray two pieces of plastic wrap generously with cooking spray and lay them gently over the pans).
Allow dough to rise again for about 45 minutes to one hour, or until risen about 1 inch above the loaf pans.
Gently removing covering.
Second rise: Cover pans with a lightweight, dry dish towel (or spray two pieces of plastic wrap generously with cooking spray and lay them gently over the pans).
Allow dough to rise again for about 45 minutes to one hour, or until risen about 1 inch above the loaf pans.
Gently removing covering.
Bake: Preheat oven to 350 F.
Bake bread for about 30-33 minutes, or until golden brown on top.
Give the top of a loaf a gentle tap
It should sound hollow.
Bake: Preheat oven to 350 F.
Bake bread for about 30-33 minutes, or until golden brown on top.
Give the top of a loaf a gentle tap
It should sound hollow.
Invert the loaves onto a wire cooling rack.
Brush the tops with butter and allow to cool for at least 10 minutes before slicing.
Invert the loaves onto a wire cooling rack.
Brush the tops with butter and allow to cool for at least 10 minutes before slicing.
Once cool, store in an airtight container or bag for 2-3 days at room temperature, or up to 5 days in the refrigerator.

title: Homemade Pasta - Love and Lemons
steps: Place the flour on a clean work surface and make a nest, Use your hands to gently bring the flour inward to incorporate, Continue working the dough with your hands to bring it together into a shaggy ball, Knead the dough for 8 to 10 minutes, At the beginning, the dough should feel pretty dry, but stick with it!

It might not feel like it’s going to come together, but after 8-10 minutes of kneading, it should become cohesive and smooth, If the dough still seems too dry, sprinkle your fingers with a tiny bit of water to incorporate, If it’s too sticky, dust more flour onto your work surface.

Shape the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic wrap, and let rest at room temperature for 30 minutes, Dust 2 large baking sheets with flour and set aside, Slice the dough into four pieces, Gently flatten one into an oval disk, Run the dough through the Pasta Roller Attachment or a pasta maker three times on level 1 (the widest setting), Set the dough piece onto a countertop or work surface.

Fold both short ends in to meet in the center, then fold the dough in half to form a rectangle (see photo above), Run the dough through the pasta roller three times on level 2, three times on level 3, and one time each on levels 4, 5, and 6.

Lay half of the pasta sheet onto the floured baking sheet and sprinkle with flour before folding the other half on top, Sprinkle more flour on top of the second half, Every side should be floured so that your final pasta noodles won't stick together.

Repeat with remaining dough, Run the pasta sheets through the Pasta Cutter Attachment (pictured is the fettuccine cutter), Repeat with remaining dough, Cook the pasta in a pot of salted boiling water for 1 to 2 minutes, How to Make Veggie Noodles, How to Make Zucchini Noodles, Vegan Mac and Cheese, Rainbow Orzo Salad, Broccoli Pasta Salad, How to Cook Spaghetti Squash

Valerie from Loveandlemons 03.01.2026I used your recipe and my new attachment for my kitchen aid, My pasta was still chewy and thick, What did I mess up ?, The very first time I made pasta it was perfect, Now, the third time it’s chewy & thick please advise.

Sicerely, Valerie Reply ↓, Ann 03.01.2026Can these be dried?, If so how?, Can they be dried in a nest?, Will they stick together well drying?, If so how do I stop that?, Reply ↓, Christy G 02.22.2026 Doubled the recipe.

Turned out perfect!, I freeze the extra nests since it’s only good 1 or 2 days in the fridge, Reply ↓ Jeanine Donofrio 02.25.2026I’m glad you loved it!, Reply ↓, Jeanine Donofrio 02.25.2026I’m glad you loved it!, Reply ↓, Lindsey 02.20.2026 I loved make homemade pasta, My family was so impressed that I made it my dad said it was the best pasta ever.

This recipe was so good everyone should try it, Reply ↓ Jeanine Donofrio 02.25.2026Aww, I’m happy it was so impressive!, Reply ↓, Jeanine Donofrio 02.25.2026Aww, I’m happy it was so impressive!, Reply ↓, RW 02.15.2026I made this pasta recipe exactly as written – did not need to add water.

It came out perfect – easy to go through the pasta maker, The second time I made it, I doubled the recipe and that was fine as well, I put the flour etc. in a bowl, Made a well for the eggs, And just worked around the bowl, Incorporating the flour into the center

Until it was mixed well, Then put it on my bread board with a tiny amount of flour to get started, And kneaded for 10 min, It doesn’t take long for it to come together, So easy when you get the hang of it, Thanks for a great recipe and instructions.

Reply ↓ Jeanine Donofrio 02.16.2026I’m glad your pasta came out so well!, Reply ↓, Jeanine Donofrio 02.16.2026I’m glad your pasta came out so well!, Reply ↓, KJ 02.14.2026 I think due to shrinkflation, four large eggs are needed, I did one batch with three and another with four, and the one with four turned out perfect!

Chewy, bouncy and delicious, Paired with homemade sauce and it was a delicious, satisfying meal, Reply ↓, Susan 02.10.2026 This was my first time making homemade pasta and it came out great, I had a hard time starting-the eggs dripped out over the flour

I ended up just smushing everything together in a hurry but the dough came together with a lot of kneading, I loved running it through the pasta maker, I won’t try any other recipes since this one worked so well.
Reply ↓ Phoebe Moore (L&L Recipe Developer) 02.13.2026I’m so glad you loved it, Susan!, Reply ↓, Phoebe Moore (L&L Recipe Developer) 02.13.2026I’m so glad you loved it, Susan!, Reply ↓

rawInstructions: Place the flour on a clean work surface and make a nest.
Add the eggs, olive oil, and salt to the center and use a fork to gently break up the eggs, keeping the flour walls intact as best as you can.
Use your hands to gently bring the flour inward to incorporate.
Continue working the dough with your hands to bring it together into a shaggy ball.
Knead the dough for 8 to 10 minutes.
At the beginning, the dough should feel pretty dry, but stick with it!
It might not feel like it’s going to come together, but after 8-10 minutes of kneading, it should become cohesive and smooth.
If the dough still seems too dry, sprinkle your fingers with a tiny bit of water to incorporate.
If it’s too sticky, dust more flour onto your work surface.
Shape the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic wrap, and let rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Dust 2 large baking sheets with flour and set aside.
Slice the dough into four pieces.
Gently flatten one into an oval disk.
Run the dough through the Pasta Roller Attachment or a pasta maker three times on level 1 (the widest setting).
Set the dough piece onto a countertop or work surface.
Fold both short ends in to meet in the center, then fold the dough in half to form a rectangle (see photo above).
Run the dough through the pasta roller three times on level 2, three times on level 3, and one time each on levels 4, 5, and 6.
Lay half of the pasta sheet onto the floured baking sheet and sprinkle with flour before folding the other half on top.
Sprinkle more flour on top of the second half.
Every side should be floured so that your final pasta noodles won't stick together.
Repeat with remaining dough.
Run the pasta sheets through the Pasta Cutter Attachment (pictured is the fettuccine cutter).
Repeat with remaining dough.
Cook the pasta in a pot of salted boiling water for 1 to 2 minutes.

RW 02.15.2026I made this pasta recipe exactly as written – did not need to add water.
It came out perfect – easy to go through the pasta maker.
The second time I made it, I doubled the recipe and that was fine as well.

I put the flour etc. in a bowl, made a well for the eggs, and just worked around the bowl, incorporating the flour into the center, until it was mixed well, then put it on my bread board with a tiny amount of flour to get started, and kneaded for 10 min.
It doesn’t take long for it to come together.
You can feel the texture change while you are kneading – to become smoother.
So easy when you get the hang of it.
Thanks for a great recipe and instructions.

Jeanine Donofrio 02.16.2026I’m glad your pasta came out so well!

KJ 02.14.2026 I think due to shrinkflation, four large eggs are needed.
I did one batch with three and another with four, and the one with four turned out perfect!
Chewy, bouncy and delicious.
Paired with homemade sauce and it was a delicious, satisfying meal.

stepsSimple: Place the flour on a clean work surface and make a nest, Use your hands to gently bring the flour inward to incorporate, Continue working the dough with your hands to bring it together into a shaggy ball, Knead the dough for 8 to 10 minutes, At the beginning, the dough should feel pretty dry, but stick with it!

It might not feel like it’s going to come together, but after 8-10 minutes of kneading, it should become cohesive and smooth, If the dough still seems too dry, sprinkle your fingers with a tiny bit of water to incorporate, If it’s too sticky, dust more flour onto your work surface.

Shape the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic wrap, and let rest at room temperature for 30 minutes, Dust 2 large baking sheets with flour and set aside, Slice the dough into four pieces, Gently flatten one into an oval disk, Run the dough through the Pasta Roller Attachment or a pasta maker three times on level 1 (the widest setting), Set the dough piece onto a countertop or work surface.

Fold both short ends in to meet in the center, then fold the dough in half to form a rectangle (see photo above), Run the dough through the pasta roller three times on level 2, three times on level 3, and one time each on levels 4, 5, and 6.

Lay half of the pasta sheet onto the floured baking sheet and sprinkle with flour before folding the other half on top, Sprinkle more flour on top of the second half, Every side should be floured so that your final pasta noodles won't stick together.

Repeat with remaining dough, Run the pasta sheets through the Pasta Cutter Attachment (pictured is the fettuccine cutter), Repeat with remaining dough, Cook the pasta in a pot of salted boiling water for 1 to 2 minutes, How to Make Veggie Noodles, How to Make Zucchini Noodles, Vegan Mac and Cheese, Rainbow Orzo Salad, Broccoli Pasta Salad, How to Cook Spaghetti Squash

rawInstructions: Place the flour on a clean work surface and make a nest.
Add the eggs, olive oil, and salt to the center and use a fork to gently break up the eggs, keeping the flour walls intact as best as you can.
Use your hands to gently bring the flour inward to incorporate.
Continue working the dough with your hands to bring it together into a shaggy ball.
Knead the dough for 8 to 10 minutes.
At the beginning, the dough should feel pretty dry, but stick with it!
It might not feel like it’s going to come together, but after 8-10 minutes of kneading, it should become cohesive and smooth.
If the dough still seems too dry, sprinkle your fingers with a tiny bit of water to incorporate.
If it’s too sticky, dust more flour onto your work surface.
Shape the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic wrap, and let rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Dust 2 large baking sheets with flour and set aside.
Slice the dough into four pieces.
Gently flatten one into an oval disk.
Run the dough through the Pasta Roller Attachment or a pasta maker three times on level 1 (the widest setting).
Set the dough piece onto a countertop or work surface.
Fold both short ends in to meet in the center, then fold the dough in half to form a rectangle (see photo above).
Run the dough through the pasta roller three times on level 2, three times on level 3, and one time each on levels 4, 5, and 6.
Lay half of the pasta sheet onto the floured baking sheet and sprinkle with flour before folding the other half on top.
Sprinkle more flour on top of the second half.
Every side should be floured so that your final pasta noodles won't stick together.
Repeat with remaining dough.
Run the pasta sheets through the Pasta Cutter Attachment (pictured is the fettuccine cutter).
Repeat with remaining dough.
Cook the pasta in a pot of salted boiling water for 1 to 2 minutes.

RW 02.15.2026I made this pasta recipe exactly as written – did not need to add water.
It came out perfect – easy to go through the pasta maker.
The second time I made it, I doubled the recipe and that was fine as well.

I put the flour etc. in a bowl, made a well for the eggs, and just worked around the bowl, incorporating the flour into the center, until it was mixed well, then put it on my bread board with a tiny amount of flour to get started, and kneaded for 10 min.
It doesn’t take long for it to come together.
You can feel the texture change while you are kneading – to become smoother.
So easy when you get the hang of it.
Thanks for a great recipe and instructions.

title: Cheesy Tomato Omelette
steps: Whisk eggs with milk, salt, and pepper.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add diced tomatoes; cook for 2 minutes.
Pour egg mixture into pan; spread evenly.
Sprinkle grated cheese over one half of the omelette.
Cook for 2 minutes or until eggs are set.
Fold omelette in half over cheese.
Cook for another 1 minute until cheese melts.
Slide omelette onto plate.
Serve immediately with a sprinkle of pepper.

title: Spanish Omelette
steps: Crack eggs into bowl, beat well.
Wash spinach, chop finely.
Heat oil in pan over medium heat.
Add spinach, cook for 1 min.
Push spinach to one side, add eggs.
Scramble eggs gently, mix with spinach.
Season with salt and pepper.
Cook for 2 more mins, eggs should be set.
Fold omelette in half, cook for 30 sec.
Slide onto plate, serve hot.
Enjoy your meal!
